vn.title: 9-nine- Haruiro Harukoi Harunokaze
vn.notes: I actually ended up enjoying Haruka way more than I thought I would. I usually find ojou-sama characters somewhat insufferable, but they balance it so well here that it's hard not to love her. She's a sweetie.

title.original: 9-nine-ここのつここのかここのいろ

vn.title: 9-nine- Kokonotsu Kokonoka Kokonoiro
vn.notes: A really promising start here! It heavily reminds me of A Clockwork Ley-line and that's not a bad thing at all. Can't wait to see what new artifacts and powers lie in store in the coming episodes.

vn.title: 9-nine- Yukiiro Yukihana Yukinoato

label.label: Petite Heroine (Multiple Routes or Main Route)

vn.notes: Yuuki Noa is definitely the cutest of the main cast, but it wasn't until this "final" installment that that was solidified in my mind. I loved that she feels like the "true route" even though the world is made up of many possible branches which are all equally true. Great finale to a great series.

vn.title: Ano Hi no Kimi o Furimukasete.

label.label: Petite Heroine (Multiple Routes or Main Route)

vn.notes:
So far surprisingly pleasant! I knew I would at least like it because Yuu is cute as hell, but the story is not half bad!
Alright, I take that back... Sort of. I was puzzled by a few developments that felt unfinished, but it turns out it was all for a reason: the ending, of course. That said... really? This trope? Aw, man... It's still a decent read. Cute girls.
Unfortunately, due to poor story development, I'm going to have to give this a lower score than I'd like to. Yuu is really cute though. Love her voice actress.

vn.title: Flyable Heart

label.label: Petite Heroine (Multiple Routes or Main Route)

vn.notes:
I fuckin' love this BGM... Also, MAYURI-SAMA, PLEASE STEP ON ME!
Man... this is some of the best character writing I've seen so far in VNs. Shou feels very much like his own person, and the relationships he develops with the very different heroines all feel natural and true-to-character despite their idiosyncrasies. Last thought: I absolutely adored true route. It was worthy of it. Giving this one a 9 because if I think hard enough, I'll probably discover a plot hole or two, but the emotional weight of the story carries hard. Each route (even Amane's) had a whole lot of heart.
I love this game. I love Unison Shift: Blossom.

label.label: Petite Heroine (Token Loli or Flat Chested Girl)

vn.title: FORTUNE ARTERIAL

label.label: Petite Heroine (Multiple Routes or Main Route)

vn.notes: Can't say that I loved the themes of the game, but I enjoyed it overall despite not entirely agreeing with the sentiment of the author. Perhaps it really boils down to how the west views toxic relationships and family vs. how the east views them. That undoubtedly will color your overall view of the game. That said, I also wish that the routes connected more heavily. The only routes that truly matter are Erica and Kiriha's routes. There was also a lot of story that could have been cut down to produce the same effect. Again, that said, great art, fun and interesting girls (for the most part), and an ending that might make you shed a few tears even if they're not entirely deserved. This wasn't the best first impression of August as a developer, but I still have high hopes for Aiyoku no Eustia. 'Til then, August!

See my review for my full thoughts on this one. To summarize my review a la ChatGPT:
A charming but painfully safe romance VN with formulaic writing, weak erotic scenes, and an over-glorified protagonist—redeemed somewhat by its heartwarming moments and easygoing Japanese.

vn.title: Nanairo Reincarnation

label.label: Petite Heroine (Token Loli or Flat Chested Girl)

vn.notes: Calling this complete. I did Kotori (both endings) and Iyo routes. While I am somewhat interested in Azusa, I have almost zero interest in Yumi (sorry Yumi fans). I might be missing out, but I'll never know, and that's ok with me. I enjoyed it regardless! Knowing how it all ends somewhat defeats the purpose in playing through subsequent routes without enough interest in the side heroines. This game reinforced what I already knew about games with "central heroines". Nanairo Drops taught me this first. Oh well. Kotori was great! I'll be honest though. I was hoping for more of a battle shounen type vibe base on the first 2 hours of play. I knew absolutely nothing prior, just that it was extremely well received. It didn't live up to the high expectations I had for it, so unfortunately that's a point off. All said and done, easy 8. Very worthy of a playthrough. One more passing complaint though..... Why didn't Iris get a scene to all to herself?! What the fuck? One last though for real this time: I was easily able to see the twist coming... This was a big factor in my ability to enjoy the story. Overall lowered its impact. Still heartbreaking though. I cry easy.

vn.title: Watashi ga Suki nara "Suki" tte Itte!

label.label: Petite Heroine (Token Loli or Flat Chested Girl)

vn.notes: I loved the dating sim style of play at first, but by the end, I have to admit I was pretty done with it. There's still quite a few SoL events that I have yet to see and probably never will, but that's the nature of this game's systems for better or worse... That said, I enjoyed my time with this game a great deal. Most routes were at least fulfilling even if some weren't the best (Spoiler: Ayame might disappoint you and it's not even her fault. It was just written that badly.) Rinka's true route was the best route of the game and made all the small bumps in the road worth it. While her route is not my favorite ever, it was extremely well done and kind of leaves me scratching my head when looking back at other routes. Worth if you have time to spare (it's a long game...)

title.original: ヨスガノソラ

vn.title: Yosuga no Sora

label.label: Petite Heroine (Multiple Routes or Main Route)

vn.notes: This one is old but gold. I see why people loved it back in the day, but I also see what people didn't like so much about it. While the drama is good and mostly believable, it could be a bit much at times. Sora is... not a great person. A good character, but not a good person--and consequently, not a good heroine. Sora is really pretty. Sincerely, she checks most of my boxes when it comes to aesthetics, but her personality is pretty awful. Even with the climax in her route, I wasn't totally sold on her. Perhaps the fandisk helps iron out some of what I'm feeling, but at the same time, I can't help but feel that it is what it is. This is Sora. Love her or hate her. She changed... A bit. I was also a little put off by Haruka who couldn't decide if he wanted to be your typical hetare or a chad. His vacillation, while understandable and somewhat believable, was a source of annoyance. All said and done though--with consideration for Akira's and Nao's routes as well--I liked it a good deal. Worth the time if you have it.

vn.title: Yumemiru Koi no Musubikata

label.label: Petite Heroine (Multiple Routes or Main Route)

vn.notes: Calling this one finished even though I don't consider it finished. I'm stopping in the middle of Isuzu's route. While Ataragi-senpai's route was pretty cute and had a lot of obvious effort put into it. I didn't feel nearly as much of that effort in the Koeda or Isuzu routes. It's a shame, but I'm just not enjoying it as much as I did common and Ataragi-senpai. If I'd known, I would have stopped there and considered it complete. As it is now, it's leaving a sour taste in my mouth to leave in the middle of a route...

title.original: ココロのカタチとイロとオト

vn.title: Kokoro no Katachi to Iro to Oto
vn.notes: Didn't spend too much time on certain scenes; This was my 12th VN played in Japanese - averaging 320字/min when I'm focused (ADHD)

title.original: ココロのカタチとイロとオト ダウンロード版

release.title: Kokoro no Katachi to Iro to Oto - Download Edition

review.text:
This game gave me a panic attack.
I've never in my life had a panic attack but knew what it was as soon as it started. For some reason that I can't yet completely process, the events leading up to the final choice left me literally gasping for air.
While quite a few sections of this game dragged and had me questioning whether it was worth continuing to play, I'm glad I did continue. I guess I feel like I was forced to confront aspects of myself that I'd left in the space under my bed.
Again, I haven't yet finished, but for all its faults, the ending is worth it.
8/10
-----
Coming back to this after a few months time just to add a couple of extra thoughts. First off, I'd like to revise my score to 7/10. After taking an objective look at the game as a whole, I find it to be a bit lacking. [spoiler]Sumire is not, in fact, the central heroine as you would think the title of the game implies. That role would belong to Akari. While I love what they did with her "route", I hate that it had to take treading over--not only Sumire--but Hinahime as well. The story is linear with one route blending into the next. Sumire, his new step sister, is not exactly a "romantic" interest. For both parties, the sex they had was an act of lust. That didn't sit well with me, especially when I discovered that routes would blend into each other. I almost stopped playing somewhere in the middle of Hinahime's route. That said, I'm glad I continued. Akari's story made the slog worth it.[/spoiler] Not too heavy on spoilers, but I took precautions regardless and gave my review some spoiler tags.
New score, 7/10.

vn.original: キミのとなりで恋してる！

review.text:
I saved Chibana for last and have not yet played her route, but I thought I'd go ahead and give a heads up to any future readers:
While this VN doesn't have a "true route". I'd say that Nagisa is the central heroine, so if you care about the order in which you read through each route, I'd save her for last. The ED is more of a movie than an ED and the game start screen changes after completion.
Overall, it's been a pretty enjoyable experience. I honestly am not the biggest Nagisa fan, but that's not to say she's a bad girl. I'd say that each girl is surprisingly likeable in their own way. That's not usual in my opinion. There's usually a girl or two who doesn't really strike me as anything special. I can confidently say that Chibana, Nagisa, and (especially) Rina all captured my attention.
This isn't an original story by any means, but it was great at doing what it set out to do. It's a futsuu no ikurademo aru rabukome. And that's not a bad thing.

Honestly... I should probably give this one a 6 or lower, but I'm giving it a 6.5. I want to give it a lower score because I feel like the game had promise that it never realized. I only ended up doing three routes--Setsu, Tamae-sensei, and Shino--but after having seen these three, I already know how the others will end, and that's because the writer plays things way too safe.
Half of the routes have no true conflict or sense of danger. They're all written with the same cookie-cutter pattern of a plot. Light hearted SoL --> Minor trouble/changing feelings --> Confession --> Major trouble (hah) --> Resolution/sex --> Roll credits --> Where are they now? You could argue this is the formula for most bishoujo games, but this is just a little too standard. Even the "main" heroine didn't feel all that much like a main heroine.
Not to mention, each route has only one sex scene, and boy, are they not exactly the best. While the art--from CGs to sprites--were decent throughout (some being really pretty!) the erotic prose was pretty bad. I found myself skipping over huge chunks of it. The best part? When Koutarou ejaculates, the most out-of-fucking-left-field sound effect plays and takes you straight out the scene. I laughed my ass off every time. It's atrocious.
I was going to make a bigger deal out of this next gripe than I will, but after having read a few of the routes, it's understandable at least. To put it frankly, the writer is constantly glazing my boy Koutarou. They don't let Koutarou's character speak for itself and let the reader infer his characterization. They instead--at any and all possible opportunity--have a side character stroke his metaphorical cock. You could say that it was a bit much. Point is, by continually drawing so much undue attention to his character, he felt increasingly unrealistic as a protagonist. I'm not saying popular protagonists are unrealistic, but I am saying that protagonists without flaws are boring.
All of that said... I did still really enjoy the game for what it is. The romance is believable. That alone makes this worth a playthrough for at least your favorite girl. Again, there's no point in playing through every route. Play through in the order of girls you like most and stop when your interest level declines. There is no multiple route mystery to unfurl here, so don't torture yourself with trying to find a "true route". It doesn't exist.
But there are a lot of heartwarming moments. I found myself smiling quite often while playing and had a few pretty good laughs. If nothing else, it was decent Japanese practice. The language is extremely easy to understand provided you have the vocab to back it up. However, you might notice quite a few bugs, typos, and errors while playing...
At one point, during Shino's route, a cell phone rang and didn't stop ringing until a cell phone rang again... Thank God it was only 10 minutes.
Anywho, Tamae-sensei best girl, hands down. Oh, and Choko's a [spoiler]hoe[/spoiler]!
Happy reading!

Can't say that I loved the themes of the game, but I enjoyed it overall despite not entirely agreeing with the sentiment of the author. Perhaps it really boils down to how the west views toxic relationships and family vs. how the east views them. That undoubtedly will color your overall view of the game. That said, I also wish that the routes connected more heavily. The only routes that truly matter are Erica and Kiriha's routes. There was also a lot of story that could have been cut down to produce the same effect. Again, that said, great art, fun and interesting girls (for the most part), and an ending that might make you shed a few tears even if they're not entirely deserved. This wasn't the best first impression of August as a developer, but I still have high hopes for Aiyoku no Eustia. 'Til then, August!

Going to go ahead and give a short review for this one since I'm a single route away from completion, and I've pretty much made up my mind.
To start, I love Tamaki Ichikawa's scenario writing. I loved the Ley-Line series which introduced me to her, Flyable Heart (which I'm currently translating), and I even enjoyed Nanatsuiro Drops. I haven't yet read Komorebi... and now, after reading Peace@Pieces, I'm less sure I'll enjoy it.
Ichikawa-sensei's characters are lovely. And her MCs all have their own unique charm that separates them from self-insert slop content. But here, in Peace@Pieces, her world-building felt lacking. I'm sure if were to have read this without knowing how well she can handle world-building, especially in the Ley-Line series, it would have been at least an 8/10 experience. This story, though, feels a bit hollow in comparison. The big baddy isn't sufficiently foreshadowed, powers aren't explained, and even some endings leave you wondering why things turned out the way they did. All of the supernatural elements inevitably end up feeling tacked on. It would have been just as good if it were a normal school-life SoL. It may have actually been even better. The heroines wouldn't have been forced into awkward situations in the middle of the common route if it were just a normal story.
All of this to say: I still like this game a lot. The characters are fun. The designs are cute. The romance feels intimate. But Ichikawa-sensei didn't fully capitalize on the supernatural elements. When you compare this to Ley-Line, which is FULL of the supernatural, this pales in comparison. She really grew as a writer over the years.
Also, I'm bagging on you for, like, your second work, but PLEASE come back to us! I need another Tamaki Ichikawa story!
---
EDIT:
Okay... So I should have read the last route (Maririn) before writing my review. I'm leaving it as-is, above, but it earned itself another half a point. Maririn's route, I knew, going in, that it would be a joke ending, but damn... that was hilarious. It kinda also gave me a new perspective on everything that came before.
Basically, I was looking for something that this game is not. This game is first and foremost a comedy. Go in for the laughs, come out satisfied.

I'm being a bit generous with my review, but there's why. This game was great. Very cute characters, cute setup, decent execution... But the romance doesn't land all the way. Melissa gives Albert too much undue attention/tsundere affection giving things a slight NTR vibe. And Miu is overall pretty whiny while looking kind of plain. Emiru is the only really solid route. I did enjoy hers a lot.
That said, the h-scenes were all wonderful. Fantastic poses. Great faces. Very hot. Not as dynamic as a lot of modern titles but great nonetheless. You'll also either love or hate how short the scenes are--lasting no more than about 30 lines each. You'll realize why they're so short pretty quickly though... I don't think the writer knew how to write a good sex scene. Very bland prose which simply describes the act. VAs carry h-scenes. Like, those voices are pretty high-tier. These scenes are so good--and with two hard-to-find flat-chested heroines to boot*--that I'm probably rating it higher than I should. *Seriously, there are surprisingly few petite main heroines in VNs...
Also, saw one Japanese guy leave a review on Fanza that I completely agree with. Why give the "main heroine" the least number of scenes? She lacks two compared to the others. And personally, Melissa did not feel enough like a central heroine. She feels like a potential side quest more than anything. And after the setup they gave for her at the beginning of the game? Yeah, that's gotta be a crime. I was prepared to cry my eyes out in what could have been a decent nakige if it had chosen to go that route.
Melissa as a character is probably divisive, but there's not enough English natives who have played this to say for certain. She lives up to the title of the game: she's selfish. But that selfishness is her endearing appeal. That's how the writer frames things at least, and that's the sense I get myself. I wish I loved her more than Emiru.
Hoping that things either improve or get even better as I start reading Chu x Chu Idol which apparently is listed as a direct sequel. Fingers crossed!

vn.original: 夜巡る、ボクらの迷子教室

review.vn: Yoru Meguru, Bokura no Maigo Kyoushitsu
review.text: Great premise, great common route, but boy did they screw the pooch with the routes. Hayate's route was fine overall. I consider it a "true route" of sorts since she's the obvious pick of the three. Riko's route was... something. I personally really like lolis and thoughtful stories involving them (think Hoshimemo or Astral Air), but this was unnecessarily convoluted with its excessive metaphors and figures of speech. It felt like talking in circles. I think the core of the story was okay, but the execution was... off? Riko, a very precocious young girl, helps guide Haruki in finding the answer to his inner turmoil. The answers are born within him without much external friction. Also, not once in the story is his relationship with Riko revealed. That wasn't at all a consideration of the author. In fact, it was actively avoided. Even though it should have been the most obvious point of contention? Well, whatever. Riko's route still had great "scenes". Unfortunately, outside of the common route with a pretty OP, the h-scenes were the next best thing. After completing Hayate, then Riko, I didn't have it in me to continue on to Kina's route even though other reviews said it was enjoyable. I was here mainly for Riko. Decent enough game, but...

vn.original: とっぱら～ざしきわらしのはなし～

review.text:
Since there aren't any reviews yet, I'll do the honors for those looking for an opinion.
*Disclaimer: I read this in Japanese language. Sachiko is cute.*
Toppara is a--when all is said and done--exactly what it's title suggests it is: a story about a zashiki-warashi. This is both its strength and a flaw. Each route always loops back to clearing up Keiji's feelings regarding Midori, the zashiki-warashi this story is about. This isn't necessarily a problem. It's been done before, and effectively. I don't think it's super effective here. Especially when this is reiterated eight times through each heroine's route. Another reason this isn't effective is because Midori doesn't receive a proper route of her own. Midori is framed as the "true route", but every other route received more attention than Midori. The writer probably intends to justify this but claiming that Midori [spoiler]is a combination of both Mikage and Sachiko but Midori feels like her own person. There are definitely elements of Mikage and Sachiko in Midori, but they're not enough like Midori. Sachiko comes much closer than Mikage though--both being rough around the edges/tomboyish. That said, it still feels like we lost the two of them by having Midori come back as she did... I don't hate it, but it could have been better.[/spoiler]
Most routes left me feeling pretty good with the exception of [spoiler]Isora[/spoiler]. Her route went off the deep end about halfway through. The writer could not stop preaching about treating the environment with respect. Not against that! But damn, dude. Give it a rest. I had to read a two hour long speech.
Touka's route also stood out for being very heavy in archaic speech, history, and mythology. This made the route exceptionally difficult compared to all the others. Heads up.
I think I'll leave discussion of routes at that.
My main gripe with the game is... Keiji. He's probably up there with the worst protagonists I've encountered in recent memory. Keiji's *[i]wadakamari[/i], for lack of a better word, is understandable but doesn't quite reach the level of empathy the writer asks for. He's suffered a tragedy, sure, but his blindness to the world around him due to that tragedy comes across as immature. It makes him an unreliable narrator.
*Tangled mass of a unresolved feelings
He's also rude--to an annoying degree. Many VNs have found success with stories featuring brusque protagonists, but Keiji doesn't come across as large and in charge. He comes across as a bit of a wuss with a mean streak. Nobody finds that endearing. This especially annoyed me in Sachiko's route where [spoiler]he chased Sachiko around with a beckoning cat when she clearly hates it. Surprise: it actually hurts her! Like, a lot![/spoiler]
He also constantly treats the youkai he encounters as subhuman. Not overtly, but implicitly. This is justified by the writer who emphasizes, through Keiji, the divide between the affairs of youkai vs. humans. The writer attempted to write a "realistic" youkai story, complete with hours of explanation and mythology, but stopped short of dark, making Keiji come off as plain discriminatory.
[spoiler]Again, this prejudice is resolved by falling in love with the youkai girls, realizing that he was the one at fault along, and blah, blah, blah...[/spoiler]
To sum up my thoughts on Keiji: he's a kid. A snot-nosed one. But I can't hate him. Because he's just a dumb kid.
I still liked this game a great deal. The art is fantastic. Most of the heroines were cute. I just don't know that the payoff is great enough. FAVORITE just did this better. Therefore, 7/10. Good, but on the average side. May not be worth your time (it's loooong), but go for it if you like the art.
